clinical note: 55M, T2DM, HTN, presented with polyuria, polydipsia x1 week, glucose 28 mmol/L, ketones 3+, ABG: pH 7.22, pCO2 28, HCO3 12. Dx: DKA. IV insulin infusion, IV fluid resuscitation, electrolyte monitoring.

clinical note: 28F, no PMHx, presented with sudden onset severe headache, neck stiffness, photophobia, vomiting, fever 39.2C, LP: turbid fluid, WBC 800 (poly), glucose 1.2, protein 3.8. Dx: bacterial meningitis. IV ceftriaxone + dexamethasone started. Neuro consult.

clinical note: 70M, CKD4, T2DM, HTN, presented with rapidly progressive renal failure, Cr 7.2 from baseline 3.1 over 3 weeks, oliguria, peripheral edema ++, K 6.2, bicarb 14. Renal US: bilateral small echogenic kidneys. Nephrology consult, HD initiated. Dx: ESRD requiring emergent dialysis.
